displayTitle: When the Alarm Has to Touch the Part
kicker: ET LEVEL I // MARKING, SORTING, CUTOFF

beats.narration: On a production line nobody reads a screen. Product flies through the coil; the alarm gate has milliseconds to act.

payload.title: ALARM TO ACTION
payload.sub: On an automated line, the output is a decision on the product.

beats.narration: The relay drives hardware that acts on the product itself. Three families: mark the flaw, sort whole pieces, or cut the bad section.

tiles.s: Paint or ink on the flaw

beats.narration: It all hangs on transport delay. The device sits downstream, so output fires after distance divided by line speed. An encoder keeps timing honest.

payload.heading: Coil to action, one travel distance later
payload.note: DELAY = DISTANCE ÷ LINE SPEED · ENCODER TRACKS TRAVEL

beats.narration: A proper sorter fails safe: losing air or power can never wave a part into the accept bin. Prove the chain dynamically at line speed.

payload.heading: The rules that back every output

beats.narration: The deliverable is not a clean screen. It is correct disposition: every bad section marked, diverted, or removed, every good piece untouched.

payload.sub: Correct disposition, not a clean screen.
